proposal.signal_logic: Starting point for "Real Frozen Six". The thesis is to expand upon the currently deployed live autonomous strategy for stocks:  Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1. Talon needs to mimic that entire strategy, with flexibility in mind for continuous improvements, but with the addition of many different groups of 6 stocks. Current we only trade AVGO, NVDA, AAPL, LLY, META and COS, but we should seek different sleeves. We need to keep in mind what this original group has in common to maintain fidelity between the 6 chosen stocks but this can increase our era robustness as certain groups may perform better under different market regimes. We will need to expand further on how to select stocks for the new groups but I’m curious to see what talon can produce from the beginning. Codex should translate this thesis into testable signal code after the operator reviews and tightens the draft.

universe.description: Configured large-cap U.S. equity universe with liquid Alpaca-tradable names.

proposal.entry_rules: Enter positions only when the thesis signal is active, liquidity is acceptable, and the configured universe member passes the benchmark/regime filter.
proposal.exit_rules: Exit positions when the thesis signal fails, the benchmark/regime filter flips, the holding window expires, or the risk guard is breached.

risk_model.description: Use small equal-weight positions, cap concentration per name, and block deployment until the experiment plan produces enough trades for a decisive verdict.

sweep_params.description: Start with a narrow smoke sweep; expand only after the first run shows enough trade count and clean data coverage.

acceptance_criteria.other: Operator should tighten these acceptance criteria before approval.

decisive_verdict_rules.pass: Passes only if it beats the benchmark after meeting the minimum trade-count and evaluated-days thresholds.
decisive_verdict_rules.inconclusive: Inconclusive if trade count, evaluated days, or runnable-era coverage is below the evidence threshold.
decisive_verdict_rules.fail: Fails if it underperforms the benchmark or breaches risk limits after evidence thresholds are met.
